elements.sent-2411-001: In this paper, we study several theoretical and numerical questions concerning the null controllability problems for linear parabolic equations and systems for several dimensions.
elements.sent-2411-002: The control is distributed and acts on a small subset of the domain.
elements.sent-2411-003: The main goal is to compute numerically a control that drives a numerical approximation of the state from prescribed initial data exactly to zero.
elements.sent-2411-004: We introduce a methodology for solving numerical controllability problems that is new in some sense.
elements.sent-2411-005: The main idea is to apply classical Lagrangian and Augmented Lagrangian techniques to suitable constrained extremal formulations that involve unbounded weights in time that make global Carleman inequalities possible.
elements.sent-2411-006: The theoretical results are validated by satisfactory numerical experiments for spatially $2$D and $3$D problems.
elements.sent-2411-007: Let~$\Omega\subset \mathbb{R ^d$ ($d\geq 1$) be a bounded domain with boundary~$\partial \Omega$ of class~$C^2$ and let~$T>0$ be given.}

elements.sent-2411-124: In this section, we use more or less standard results from convex analysis to reformulate~\eqref{extremal:problem:02 appropriately and deduce related efficient algorithms.}

elements.sent-2411-126: For every~$R>0$, we will consider the family of perturbations~$\{\Phi_R(\cdot\,,p)\}_{p\in L^2(Q)}$, defined as follows: \begin{equation}\Phi_R(v,p):=\overline{F}_R(Mv-p)+G(v) \quad \forall v\in U,\ \ \forall p\in L^2(Q).\end{equation}.

elements.sent-2411-165: In this section, we will indicate how to solve~\eqref{truncated:problem using {\it Uzawa's algorithm.}}

elements.sent-2411-167: Thus, let us set \begin{equation}J_R^*(q):=\Phi_R^*(0,q)=\dfrac{1}{2}\iint_{Q} (\rho_R^{-2}|q|^2+\rho_0^{-2}|M^*q|^2 +2q\overline{y})\,dx\,dt \quad \forall q\in L^2(Q)\end{equation} and let us rewrite the dual problem~\eqref{extremal:problem:03 in the form \begin{equation}\label{problem:mathcalJ}  Minimize J_R^*(q) Subject to q\in L^2(Q). \end{equation} We note that~$\mathcal{J _R^*$ is a quadratic functional.}}

elements.sent-2411-168: More precisely, one has \begin{equation}J_R^*(q)=\dfrac{1}{2}a_R(q,q) + \ell (q) \quad \forall q \in L^2(Q),\end{equation} where~$a_R(\cdot\,,\cdot)$ and~$\ell(\cdot)$ are respectively given by \begin{equation}a_R(q,q'):=\iint_{Q} (\rho_R^{-2}qq'+\rho_0^{-2}M^*qM^*q')\,dx\,dt, \quad \ell(q'):=\iint_{Q} \overline{y} q' \,dx\,dt .\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-169: The bilinear form~$a_R(\cdot\,,\cdot)$ is symmetric and coercive on~$L^2(Q)\times L^2(Q)$.
elements.sent-2411-170: Consequently, it is completely natural and appropriate to apply Uzawa's algorithm, denoted ALG~1 in this paper.
elements.algorithm-2411-001: \caption{Uzawa's Algorithm (ALG~1, optimal step gradient version).} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Procedure{Uzawa01}{$\overline{y},q^0,R,itmax,tol$} \State Set~$err\leftarrow 1$ and~$k\leftarrow 0$. \While{$k \leq itmax$ and~$err\geq tol$ } \State Compute $d^k\leftarrow \rho_R^{-2}q^k+M(\rho_0^{-2}M^*q^k)+\overline{y}.$ \State Compute the optimal step \begin{equation}\label{rk:optimal:step} \displaystyle r_k\leftarrow \,\argmin\limits_{r\in R_+}\,J_R^*(q^k-rd^k), \nonumber q^{k+1}\leftarrow \,q^k-r_k d^k.\end{equation} \If{$\displaystyle{\|q^{k+1}-q^{k}\|_{L^2(Q)}\over\|q^k\|_{L^2(Q)}}\leq tol$} \Comment{Convergence test} \State Set~$q\leftarrow q^{k+1}$. \Else \State~$err \leftarrow \displaystyle{\|q^{k+1}-q^{k}\|_{L^2(Q)}\over\|q^k\|_{L^2(Q)}}$. \State~$k \leftarrow k+1$. \EndIf \EndWhile \State Define~$y\leftarrow -\rho_R^{-2}q$ and~$v\leftarrow \rho_0^{-2}M^*q$. \Comment{Computing the state and the control} \EndProcedure \end{algorithmic}
elements.sent-2411-171: The weights~$\rho_R$ and~$\rho_0$ play a crucial role in the convergence properties of~ALG~1.
elements.sent-2411-172: Indeed, the Fr\'echet derivative of~$\mathcal{J _R^*$ is given by \begin{equation}DJ_R^*(q)=\rho_R^{-2}q+M(\rho_0^{-2}M^*q)+\overline{y} \quad \forall q\in L^2(Q)\end{equation} and it is therefore easy to see that \begin{equation}(DJ_R^*(q)-DJ_R^*(p),q-p)_{L^2(Q)}\geq C_1(R)\|q-p\|_{L^2(Q)}^2\quad \forall p,q\in L^2(Q),\end{equation} with~$C_1(R):=\min\{\rho_R^{-2 (x,t):(x,t)\in Q\} \thicksim R^{-2}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-173: On the other hand, we have \begin{equation}\|DJ_R^*(q)-DJ_R^*(p)\|_{L^2(Q)}\leq C_0 \|q-p\|_{L^2(Q)}\quad \forall p,q\in L^2(Q),\end{equation} where~$C_0 = (\|\rho_R^{-1}\|_\infty^2 +\|\rho_0^{-2}\|_\infty^2)^{1/2}$ does not depend of~$R>0$.
elements.sent-2411-174: Thus, if we are able to find~$a_0,b_0>0$ such that the optimal steps~$r_k$ defined in~\eqref{rk:optimal:step satisfy \begin{equation}a_0\leq r_k \leq b_0 < \dfrac{2C_1(R)}{C_0^2}\thicksim R^{-2} ,\end{equation} then, from standard results concerning gradient algorithms, the convergence of~ALG~1 would be ensured.}
elements.sent-2411-175: In view of the structure of the extremal problem~\eqref{problem:mathcalJ , it is reasonable to introduce conjugate gradient iterates to improve the convergence of~ALG~1.}
elements.sent-2411-176: We will consider the so called {\it Polak-Ribi\`ere version , here denoted ALG~2.}
elements.sent-2411-177: Note that, as in the case of ALG~1, the one-dimensional extremal problems arising at each step are elementary.
elements.sent-2411-178: For ALG~2, the computational work is not much harder (just the computation of~$\gamma^k$ and the new~$d^k$) but well known results suggest a better (superlinear) convergence rate.
elements.algorithm-2411-002: \caption{Uzawa's algorithm (ALG~2, Polak-Ribi\`ere conjugate gradient version).} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Procedure{Uzawa02}{$\overline{y},q^0,R,itmax,tol$} \State Compute~$g^0\leftarrow \rho_R^{-2}q^0+M(\rho_0^{-2} M^*q^0)+\overline{y}.$ \If{$\displaystyle{\|g^0\|_{L^2(Q)}\over\|q^0\|_{L^2(Q)}}\leq tol$} \State Set~$q\leftarrow q^0$ and~$err\leftarrow 0$. \Else \begin{equation}d^0\leftarrow g^0, r_0\leftarrow \,\argmin\limits_{r\in R_+} J_R^*(q^0-rd^0), q^{1}\leftarrow \,q^0-r_0 d^0, k\leftarrow 1.\end{equation} \State Set~ $err\leftarrow 1$. \EndIf \While{$k\leq itmax$ and~$err\geq tol$} \State Compute $g^k\leftarrow\rho_R^{-2} q^k + M(\rho_0^{-2} M^*q^k) + \overline{y}$. \Comment{Steepest descent} \State Compute \Comment{Construction of the new descent direction} \begin{equation}\gamma_k\leftarrow\, \dfrac{(g^{k},g^{k}-g^{k-1})_{L^2(Q)}}{\|g^{k-1}\|_{L^2(Q)}^2}, \quad d^k\leftarrow d^{k-1} + \gamma^k g^k r_k\leftarrow \,argmin_{r\in R_+} J_R^*(q^k- rd^k), q^{k+1}\leftarrow \,q^{k} - r_k d^k.\end{equation} \If{$\frac{\|g^{k}\|_{L^2(Q)}}{\|g^0\|_{L^2(Q)}}\leq tol$} \Comment{Converge test} \State Set~$q\leftarrow q^{k}$ \Else \State~$err \leftarrow \displaystyle{\frac{\|g^{k}\|_{L^2(Q)}}{\|g^0\|_{L^2(Q)}}}$. \State Set~$k\leftarrow \, k+1$. \EndIf \EndWhile \State Set~$y\leftarrow -\rho_R^{-2}q$ and~$v\leftarrow \rho_0^{-2} M^* q$. \EndProcedure \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-003: \caption{Uzawa's algorithm (ALG~3, optimal step gradient for Augmented Lagrangian).} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Procedure{Uzawa03}{$\overline{y},q^0,R,K,itmax,tol$} \State Set~$err \leftarrow 1$ and~$k\leftarrow 0$. \State Define~$v^0\leftarrow \rho_0^{-2}M^*q^0$ and~$m^0\leftarrow \rho_R^{-2}q^0+Mv^0+\overline{y}$. \While{$k\leq itmax$ and~$err\geq tol$} \State Compute~$v^k$ and~$m^k$ solving the system \begin{equation}\left\{ {cccl} \ds \dfrac{1}{K} \rho_0^2 v + M^*m = \dfrac{1}{K} M^*q^k , \noalign{ } \ds -Mv + (1+K\rho_R^{-2}) m = \overline{y} + \rho_R^{-2}q^k ,  \right.\end{equation} \State Compute the optimal step and update \begin{equation}r_k \leftarrow argmin_{r\in R^+} J_{R,K}^*(q^k-r m^k).\end{equation} \State Compute~$q^{k+1}$ as follows: \begin{equation}q^{k+1}\leftarrow q^k-r_k m^k.\end{equation} \If{$\|q^{k+1}-q^k\|_{L^2(Q)}/\|q^k\|_{L^2(Q)}\leq tol$} \State Set~$q\leftarrow q^{k+1}$. \Else \State Compute~$err \leftarrow \|q^{k+1}-q^k\|_{L^2(Q)}/\|q^k\|_{L^2(Q)}$. \State Set~$k\leftarrow k+1$. \EndIf \EndWhile \State Define~$y\leftarrow -\rho_R^{-2}q$ and~$v\leftarrow \rho_0^{-2}M^*q$. \EndProcedure \end{algorithmic}}

elements.algorithm-2411-004: \caption{Uzawa's algorithm (ALG~4, optimal step gradient for Stokes Augmented Lagrangian.} \label{ALG4} \begin{algorithmic}[1] \Procedure{Uzawa03}{$\overline{\bm{y}},\bm{q}^0,R,K,itmax,tol$} \State Set~$err \leftarrow 1$ and~$k\leftarrow 0$. \State Define~$\bm{v}^0\leftarrow \rho_0^{-2}M^*\bm{q}^0$ and~$\bm{m}^0\leftarrow \rho_R^{-2}\bm{q}^0+M\bm{v}^0+\overline{\bm{y}}$. \While{$k\leq itmax$ and~$err\geq tol$} \State Compute~$\bm{v}^k$ and~$\bm{m}^k$ solving the system \begin{equation}\left\{ {cccl} \ds \dfrac{1}{K} \rho_0^2 v + M^*m = \dfrac{1}{K} M^*q^k , \noalign{ } \ds -Mv + (1+K\rho_R^{-2}) m = \overline{y} + \rho_R^{-2}q^k ,  \right.\end{equation} \State Compute the optimal step and update \begin{equation}r_k \leftarrow argmin_{r\in R^+} J_{R,K}^*(q^k-rm^k).\end{equation} \State Compute~$\bm{q}^{k+1}$ as follows: \begin{equation}q^{k+1}\leftarrow q^k-r_km^k.\end{equation} \If{$\|\bm{q}^{k+1}-\bm{q}^k\|_{\bm{L}^2(Q)}/\|\bm{q}^k\|_{\bm{L}^2(Q)}\leq tol$} \State Set~$\bm{q}\leftarrow \bm{q}^{k+1}$. \Else \State Compute~$err \leftarrow \|\bm{q}^{k+1}-\bm{q}^k\|_{\bm{L}^2(Q)}/\|\bm{q}^k\|_{L^2(Q)}$. \State Set~$k\leftarrow k+1$. \EndIf \EndWhile \State Define~$\bm{y}\leftarrow -\rho_R^{-2}\bm{q}$ and~$\bm{v}\leftarrow \rho_0^{-2}M^*\bm{q}$. \EndProcedure \end{algorithmic}}

elements.sent-2411-289: In this paper, we have presented several algorithms based on Lagrangian and Augmented Lagrangian formulations of null controllability problems for the heat and the Stokes PDEs.
elements.sent-2411-290: To apply the techniques, we introduced a large parameter~$R>0$ in order to truncate the weight function associated with the state variable and then a second paremater~$K>0$ that plays the role of penalization in the Augmented Lagrangian.
elements.sent-2411-291: As~$R$ goes to~$+\infty$, we recover the solution of the original problem.
elements.sent-2411-292: This is proved rigorously and has been numerically validated in several experiments.
elements.sent-2411-293: One of the main virtues of the presented methods is that it can be applied with reasonable effort to control problems for high spatial dimension PDEs, which is not so clear for other strategies.
elements.sent-2411-294: The arguments and results can be extended and adapted to many other controllability problems.
elements.sent-2411-295: Thus, they are valid for the internal or boundary control of parabolic PDEs and systems complemented with other boundary conditions, control problems in other domains, etc. In a next future, we will investigate their utility in the context of some semilinear and nonlinear problems like state-dependent diffusion heat equations, Burgers, Navier-Stokes, and Boussinesq systems, etc. The formulation and resolution of null or exact controllability problems with Lagrangian and Augmented Lagrangian methods for wave and Schr\"odinger PDEs remain, as far as we know, unexplored.
elements.sent-2411-296: This will also be investigated in forthcoming work.
